title: Bridge to Infinity — Cislunar logistics & population flow model
description: Portable parameter pack for running Earth launcher, lunar export, habitat completion, and matched-flow sanity checks. Paste into an external model or import as context. Not a forecast — illustrative scenario from the essay thesis.
source: Age of Wonders essay: Bridge to Infinity (Appendix L + Section IV throughput tables)

derived_reference_throughputs_t_per_year.notes: Payload = mass delivered to LEO per shot (essay table). Total launched mass including bridge stage is larger.

formulas.complexity_match_ratio: match = M_earth_launch_network / M_earth_complexity (values near 1 = balanced pipeline)

2150s.note: Essay table; may be conservative vs matched-flow at max lunar habitat rate — see worked_examples

2150s_matched_flow_sanity.interpretation: At 500 Mt/yr lunar and 1.5 t complexity per new resident, Earth must export ~33 Mt/yr to match ~22.2M residents/yr habitat capacity. Essay's ~10 Mt/yr row implies either lower fill rate, falling kg/person as orbital fabs mature, or parallel non-launcher lift — reconcile in scenario runs.

single_site_people_transport_upper_bound.note: Upper bound only; real system splits cargo vs crew, bridge vehicle limits, and human-rated g-load may use separate tubes.

bottleneck_variables_for_sensitivity: bridge_vehicle_flights_per_year (may be < launcher shots if vehicles are limiting)
complexity_cargo_per_new_resident_t (should decline over decades if orbital industry matures)
prompt_for_external_model: You are given bridge-logistics-model.json. Compute: (1) habitats_per_year and capacity_added for any lunar_export_t_per_year; (2) required Earth complexity export for matched flow; (3) required tubes at hourly 100t equivalent; (4) sweep N_sites and N_tubes_per_site to hit a target complexity export. Flag when Earth export table < matched complexity. Use formulas object exactly.
